title: The Polite Pitch
learningGoal: The learner will mimic native intonation curves, utilizing a rising pitch boundary at the end of requests ('Could you help me? ⤴') to acoustically convey politeness and mitigate imperative flatness.
instructions: In English, HOW you say something is just as important as what you say. If your voice is flat and loud, you sound angry! When you ask a polite question ('Could you...'), your voice should go UP at the end of the sentence. Listen to the audio and copy the joyful, rising pitch!

contentItems.text: Make your voice go up (⤴) at the end of friendly questions. Make it go down (⤵) when you are stating a fact.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप मेरी मदद कर सकते हैं?
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Raise the pitch of your voice on the word 'me'. Imagine an arrow pointing up!
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying it with a flat, robotic tone.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Pitch goes UP on 'me'. This sounds friendly.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप दरवाज़ा खोल सकते हैं?

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Gentle, smiling tone. Stretch the 'eee' in please.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप पानी बढ़ा सकते हैं?

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Voice goes DOWN. You should sound a little sad.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप कृपया प्रतीक्षा कर सकते हैं?

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Standard greeting. Pitch drops slightly at the very end.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप उसे दोहरा सकते हैं?
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Pitch goes UP heavily on 'that'. (Used when you didn't hear someone).

targetPhrasesOrWords.targetText: Excuse me, where is the bathroom?
targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: एक्सक्यूज़ मी, वेयर इज़ द बाथरूम?
targetPhrasesOrWords.syllableSplit: Ex-cuse me, where is the BATH-room⤴

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Very bright, happy voice! Pitch drops at the end.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप धीरे बोल सकते हैं?

expectedPronunciationText: Could you help me Excuse me Yes please

feedbackRules.onPass: Awesome! Your voice sounds incredibly friendly and polite. You used the rising pitch perfectly on your questions!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! You sound a little robotic or angry. Smile when you speak! When you ask 'Could you', make your pitch go UP at the end of the sentence.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for 'Excuse me'. Make it sound like a gentle question so you don't scare the person!

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: could you help me excuse me could you open the door yes please no thank you could you pass the water i'm sorry could you please wait how are you i am fine thank you could you repeat that excuse me where is the bathroom have a good day could you speak slowly nice to meet you
acceptedVariants: could you help me excuse me could you open the door yes please no thank you could you pass the water i'm sorry could you please wait how are you i am fine thank you could you repeat that excuse me where is the bathroom have a good day could you speak slowly nice to meet you
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: Requires active modulation of prosodic contours. Rising terminal intonation contour (L* H-H%) mandatory for yes/no modal requests to encode politeness.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Could you help me? Excuse me...

hintText: Smile while you speak! Smiling physically changes the shape of your mouth and raises your pitch automatically.
commonMistakes: Using a flat, descending tone for requests, which encodes aggression in English.
